topic.what: First comprehensive empirical study characterizing clandestine Iranian third-party iOS app stores
topic.how: Developed Havva system combining web crawlers, IPA collection, App Store matching, and static/dynamic analysis over 5.5 months
topic.why_matters: Documents security/privacy risks from pirated/tampered apps, revenue losses from piracy, and unintended consequences of sanctions and censorship on digital access

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total unique applications collected from three stores
characteristics.evidence: Dataset comprising apps from Store A (558), Store B (1323), and Store C (221)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of apps unavailable in official App Store
characteristics.context: Many essential for daily life but absent due to policy restrictions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Apps modified to bypass paid subscriptions
characteristics.context: Educational apps especially language learning dominate

characteristics.evidence: Could not be obtained for comparison due to FairPlay DRM
characteristics.context: Excluded from binary analysis due to cost

characteristics.evidence: Estimated $5.26 million in lost revenue from just two stores
characteristics.context: Based on displacement rates from literature (25-40%)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of apps with injected files vs App Store versions
characteristics.evidence: 360 of 530 apps with file-level differences contained crack tools or artifacts
characteristics.context: Static analysis comparing IPA file listings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Apps with unauthorized ad-hoc signing profiles
characteristics.evidence: mobileprovision files found in Iranian store IPAs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Apps containing Cydia Substrate or iGameGod
characteristics.evidence: Cydia Substrate found in 186 apps for runtime hooking
characteristics.context: Runtime capability to bypass payment/auth handlers
Only two of three stores provide download statistics

characteristics.what_it_measures: Apps present across all four platforms
characteristics.evidence: Only 20 apps appear in all Iranian stores AND App Store

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of apps never updated during study period
characteristics.evidence: Store A had 79.9% unupdated; Store B 79.1%; Store C 59.0%
characteristics.context: Significant security implications from delayed updates

characteristics.what_it_measures: Network flows sent over plaintext HTTP
characteristics.context: Not introduced by repackaging; from original app code

novelty: First comprehensive study of clandestine Iranian iOS app stores
Havva system for automated collection and analysis
Discovered morphed shell apps in official App Store
Documented compound security/privacy risks from injected libraries
Analysis of enterprise/ad-hoc distribution model exploitation
open_problems: Policy mechanisms to mitigate unintended sanctions effects
Balancing security access vs platform integrity
Impact of certificate revocations on user data loss
Effective implementation of General License D-2
Long-term sustainability of constrained app ecosystems
Re-evaluation of blanket sanctions on digital services
